question_text: A student slides a wooden block across a rough table and then across a smooth polished table. Which statement correctly compares the friction forces?

options.label: A
options.text: Friction is the same on both surfaces because the block is the same

options.label: B
options.text: Friction is greater on the rough table because the surfaces have more contact points

options.label: C
options.text: Friction is greater on the polished table because it is harder

options.label: D
options.text: Friction depends only on how fast the block is moving

explanation: Friction increases with surface roughness because rough surfaces have more irregularities that interlock and create more contact points between the block and table. A polished surface is smoother, so friction is reduced.
